lines: Step in, traveler. A proper road begins with bread in the pocket and a little courage under the ribs.
Take this travel cake. No coin today; tell the trees I keep fair measure.
That cake should keep your knees from knocking until Oldwood. Come back when we have prices, shelves, and gossip worth paying for.
Barrels are full, shelves are named, and the till is honest. The real shop counter comes next.
